steps.title: Identify the barrier effect to test
steps.content: The hypothesis states freshwater acts as a barrier preventing sea stars from climbing. To support it, the experiment must show sea stars climbing freely without freshwater but being blocked when it's present.

steps.title: Match the barrier evidence
steps.content:
The matching choice demonstrates this directly: seawater-only sea stars climb to the top, while sea stars in the freshwater tank stop just below the layer, proving the freshwater blocks them.

The other choices fail to show differential behavior between the groups, and D shows sea stars penetrating the freshwater rather than being blocked by it.

B is correct.
